mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is easing from an already restrictive level.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are not positive more often than negative during months when FEDFUNDS falls versus the prior month, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is above its recent trend while FEDFUNDS remains low, real-rate pressure and expected policy tightening increase equity discount rates, so the next 20 trading days tend to produce negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS by less than 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening labor income and risk of a demand slowdown, which should pressure earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward returns are positive in a majority of months with a month-over-month rise in UNRATE, or if the effect is absent in late-cycle episodes with unemployment above 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A threshold jump in PAYEMS indicates labor market strength and supports near-term consumption and earnings revisions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when employment growth accelerates materially.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150k in a month
failure_condition: Reject if months with PAYEMS gains above 150k do not outperform other months on 20-day forward returns, or if the hit rate falls below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST captures stronger consumer spending and residential activity, which should lift short-horizon equity returns because revenue expectations improve across cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal is not stronger than either series alone, or if positive 20-day returns do not occur in more than 55% of months where both RSAFS and HOUST rise month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO combined with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates improving real output without renewed inflation pressure, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day returns are not positive in post-shock periods where INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL growth is non-accelerating, or if the joint condition underperforms INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in GDPC1 indicates weakening aggregate demand and lower forward earnings growth, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days when GDP growth turns down materially.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 falls for 2 consecutive quarterly observations
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are positive in a majority of episodes after two consecutive quarterly GDPC1 declines, or if the signal disappears after accounting for revision-prone releases.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS weakens while UNRATE rises, the combination signals households are spending less and labor income is deteriorating, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and RSAFS falls in the same monthly observation
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not show negative 5-day returns more often than positive ones, or if the joint condition offers no improvement over UNRATE alone.
